markdown.markdown:
The course runs Volume I in weeks 1–7 (posing the General Enterprise Optimization Problem) and Volume II in weeks 8–14 (solving it). The double rule marks the volume boundary: the midterm sits exactly where the GEOP is posed. Instructors may re-balance — every chapter page is self-contained. ```{=html}
WeekChaptersTheme
1VI.1 — Introduction to Dynamic Corporate Transformation
VI.2 — Enterprise Systems and Transformation		DCT vision; enterprise systems
2VI.3 — Mathematical Foundations
VI.4 — Enterprise Modeling Principles		Mathematical foundations; modeling principles
3VI.5 — Enterprise State Representation
VI.6 — Enterprise Transformation Operators		Enterprise state; transformation operators
4VI.7 — Dynamic Enterprise Systems
VI.8 — Stochastic Enterprise Dynamics		Dynamic systems; stochastic dynamics
5VI.9 — Enterprise State Architecture
VI.10 — Enterprise Capital Architecture
VI.11 — Enterprise Performance Architecture		The architectures: state, capital, performance
6VI.12 — Enterprise Risk, Resilience, and Robustness Architecture
VI.13 — Unified Enterprise Transformation Architecture		Risk, resilience, robustness; UETA
7VI.14 — Mathematical Analysis of the Unified Enterprise Transformation Architecture
VI.15 — Mathematical Formulation of Enterprise Optimization
VI.16 — Synthesis, Future Directions, and Transition to Volume II		Analysis of UETA; the GEOP; synthesis
◆ Midterm
8VII.1 — Introduction to Enterprise Optimization
VII.2 — The General Enterprise Optimization Problem Revisited		Enterprise optimization; GEOP revisited
9VII.3 — Convex Enterprise Optimization
VII.4 — Nonlinear Enterprise Optimization		Convex; nonlinear
10VII.5 — Dynamic Enterprise Optimization
VII.6 — Optimal Control of Enterprise Systems		Dynamic optimization; optimal control (PMP)
11VII.7 — Dynamic Programming for Enterprise Systems
VII.8 — Hamilton--Jacobi--Bellman Enterprise Framework
VII.9 — Stochastic Enterprise Optimization		The Bellman arc: DP, HJB, stochastic control
12VII.10 — Neuro-Fuzzy Robust Enterprise Optimization
VII.11 — Distributionally Robust Enterprise Optimization		The robustness pair: NF-REO, DRO
13VII.12 — Multi-Objective Enterprise Optimization
VII.13 — Machine Learning for Enterprise Transformation		Multi-objective; machine learning
14VII.14 — Artificial Intelligence for Enterprise Optimization
VII.15 — Enterprise Digital Twins and Autonomous Enterprise Transformation
VII.16 — Enterprise Applications and Integrated Transformation Case Studies		AI; digital twins; case-study capstone
◆ Final
```
